annotations.label: Concept of infinity (Yajurveda)

anchor.snippet: contains the earliest concept of infinity

annotations.note: A philosophical concept, not a theorem; Mathlib has infinite cardinals (Cardinal.aleph0) but no formalization of the historical 'concept of infinity'.

annotations.label: Irrationality of the square root of two

anchor.snippet: discovers the irrationality of the square root of two

annotations.note: irrational_sqrt_two states √2 is irrational exactly.

annotations.label: Classification of numbers and types of infinity

anchor.snippet: classifying all numbers into three sets

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes infinite cardinals and their arithmetic (distinct 'sizes' of infinity) but not the Jain numerical classification itself.

annotations.label: Method of exhaustion

anchor.snippet: states the method of exhaustion for area determination

annotations.note: The method itself isn't formalized, though modern integration (intervalIntegral/MeasureTheory) is its rigorous descendant.

annotations.label: Euclid: infinitude of primes, Euclidean algorithm, fundamental theorem of arithmetic

anchor.snippet: proves the infinitude of prime numbers

annotations.note: Infinitude of primes (Nat.exists_infinite_primes), Euclidean algorithm (EuclideanDomain.gcd), and unique factorization (Nat.primeFactorsList_unique) are all in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Archimedes: area of a circle and parabola, bounds on π

anchor.snippet: that the area of a circle was equal to

annotations.note: Area of a disc is the 2-dimensional special case of the general ball-volume formula, but the parabola quadrature and π bounds aren't formalized.

annotations.label: Sieve of Eratosthenes

anchor.snippet: uses his sieve algorithm to quickly isolate prime numbers

annotations.note: The sieve algorithm isn't formalized (Nat.Prime predicate exists, but not the sieve procedure).

annotations.label: Gaussian elimination

anchor.snippet: A method of Gaussian elimination appears in the Chinese text

annotations.note: No Gaussian elimination / row-reduction algorithm is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Horner's method

anchor.snippet: Horner's method appears in the Chinese text

annotations.note: Horner's evaluation scheme isn't a formalized result (used only internally by the ring tactic).

annotations.label: Pappus's hexagon theorem and centroid theorem

anchor.snippet: states his hexagon theorem and his centroid theorem

annotations.note: Neither Pappus's hexagon theorem nor his centroid theorem is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chinese remainder theorem

anchor.snippet: The Chinese remainder theorem is developed by

annotations.note: ZMod.chineseRemainder gives the ring isomorphism ZMod (m*n) ≃ ZMod m × ZMod n for coprime m,n.

annotations.label: Definition of sine and cosine

anchor.snippet: It defines the concepts of sine and cosine

annotations.note: Real.sin and Real.cos are defined (via the complex exponential).

annotations.label: Solving indeterminate equations of the second degree

anchor.snippet: invents the method of solving indeterminate equations of the second degree

annotations.note: Solvability of Pell's equation is formalized, but not general second-degree indeterminate-equation methods.

annotations.label: Brahmagupta's identity and Brahmagupta theorem

anchor.snippet: Brahmagupta's identity , and the Brahmagupta theorem

annotations.note: The Brahmagupta(-Fibonacci) identity is formalized; the geometric Brahmagupta theorem on cyclic quadrilaterals is not.

annotations.label: Volume of a sphere

anchor.snippet: gives the rule for finding the volume of a sphere

annotations.note: Volume of a 3-ball is the dimension-3 special case of the general Euclidean ball-volume formula.

annotations.label: Newton-Gauss interpolation formula

anchor.snippet: discovers the Newton-Gauss interpolation formula

annotations.note: Finite-difference interpolation formulas are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Thābit ibn Qurra theorem on amicable numbers

anchor.snippet: discovered the theorem by which pairs of amicable numbers can be found

annotations.note: Amicable numbers and Thābit's rule are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tangent, secant and inverse trigonometric ratios

anchor.snippet: extended the Indian concepts of sine and cosine to other trigonometrical ratios

annotations.note: Real.tan, Real.arctan, Real.arcsin and Real.arccos are defined (secant is not a separately named definition).

annotations.label: Binomial theorem for integer exponents

anchor.snippet: discovered the binomial theorem for integer exponents

annotations.note: add_pow is the binomial theorem for natural-number exponents in a commutative semiring.

annotations.label: Special case of Fermat's Last Theorem

anchor.snippet: first states a special case of Fermat's Last Theorem

annotations.note: FLT for the exponent n=3 is proven in Mathlib (and n=4 separately).

annotations.label: Law of sines

anchor.snippet: Law of sines is discovered by

annotations.note: EuclideanGeometry.law_sin formalizes the law of sines for a triangle.

annotations.label: Proofs by mathematical induction

anchor.snippet: the first known proofs by mathematical induction

annotations.note: Mathematical induction is the recursor Nat.rec, generated automatically with the natural numbers (Lean core).

annotations.label: Angle addition formula for sine

anchor.snippet: sin (α + β) = sin α cos β + sin β cos α

annotations.note: Real.sin_add is exactly sin(x+y) = sin x cos y + cos x sin y.

annotations.label: Alhazen's problem

anchor.snippet: formulated and solved Alhazen's problem geometrically

annotations.note: Alhazen's optical/geometric problem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Classification of cubic equations (Khayyám)

anchor.snippet: gave a complete classification of cubic equations with geometric solutions

annotations.note: Khayyám's geometric classification of cubics is not in Mathlib (an algebraic cubic solution exists only in Archive).

annotations.label: A positive number has two square roots

anchor.snippet: the first text to recognize that a positive number has two square roots

annotations.note: That x²=a has exactly the two roots ±√a is captured by lemmas like pow_eq_pow_iff_of_ne_zero / sq_eq_sq_iff_eq_or_eq_neg.

annotations.label: Chakravala method for Pell's equation

anchor.snippet: the Chakravala method which was the first generalized solution of so-called Pell's equation

annotations.note: Existence of nontrivial Pell solutions is formalized, but not the Chakravala algorithm itself.

annotations.label: Early form of Rolle's theorem

anchor.snippet: develops early form of Rolle's theorem

annotations.note: Rolle's theorem is formalized as exists_deriv_eq_zero / exists_hasDerivAt_eq_zero.

annotations.label: Power series (Madhava / Taylor series)

anchor.snippet: discovers the power series expansion for

annotations.note: Madhava's arctan/Leibniz power series is formalized (hasSum_arctan, Real.tendsto_sum_pi_div_four), as are exp/sin/cos series.

annotations.label: Mean value theorem of differential calculus

anchor.snippet: states the mean value theorem of differential calculus

annotations.note: exists_deriv_eq_slope is the mean value theorem.

annotations.label: Series expansion for the inverse-tangent function

anchor.snippet: discovers the series expansion for the inverse-tangent function

annotations.note: hasSum_arctan gives the power-series expansion of arctangent (and Real.tendsto_sum_pi_div_four the Leibniz value).

annotations.label: Solution of the quartic equation

anchor.snippet: Lodovico Ferrari solves the quartic equation

annotations.note: An explicit quartic solution exists only in Archive (Theorems100.quartic_eq_zero_iff), not in the Mathlib library.

annotations.label: General solution of cubic equations

anchor.snippet: creating a method for solving all cubics

annotations.note: Cardano's cubic solution exists only in Archive (Theorems100.cubic_eq_zero_iff), not in the Mathlib library.

annotations.label: Kepler-Poinsot polyhedra

anchor.snippet: discovers two of the Kepler-Poinsot polyhedra

annotations.note: Kepler–Poinsot star polyhedra are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area under a cycloid

anchor.snippet: the area under a cycloid is three times the area of its generating circle

annotations.note: The cycloid area result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fermat's Last Theorem (claim)

anchor.snippet: claims to have proven Fermat's Last Theorem

annotations.note: The statement FermatLastTheorem is defined and cases n=3,4 are proven, but the general theorem is not proven in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Descartes' theorem

anchor.snippet: develops Descartes' theorem

annotations.note: Descartes' rule of signs is formalized; if 'Descartes' theorem' instead means the tangent-circle theorem, that is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Length of a cycloid

anchor.snippet: the length of a cycloid is four times the diameter of its generating circle

annotations.note: The cycloid arc-length result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of calculus

anchor.snippet: works on the fundamental theorem of calculus

annotations.note: Both differentiation and evaluation forms of the FTC are formalized.

annotations.label: Infinite series for the logarithm

anchor.snippet: discover an infinite series for the logarithm

annotations.note: The Mercator/Newton power series for the logarithm is formalized.

annotations.label: Gregory series for inverse tangent

anchor.snippet: develops a series expansion for the inverse- tangent function

annotations.note: The Gregory/Madhava arctangent series is formalized as hasSum_arctan.

annotations.label: Taylor's theorem

anchor.snippet: James Gregory discovers Taylor's theorem

annotations.note: Taylor's theorem with Lagrange (and Cauchy) remainder is formalized.

annotations.label: Separation of variables

anchor.snippet: discovers the technique of separation of variables

annotations.note: The ODE solving technique of separation of variables is not formalized as a result.

annotations.label: L'Hôpital's rule

anchor.snippet: states his rule for the computation of certain limits

annotations.note: L'Hôpital's rule is formalized in several forms.

annotations.label: Brachistochrone problem

anchor.snippet: solve the brachistochrone problem

annotations.note: The brachistochrone variational problem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bernoulli numbers

anchor.snippet: Seki Takakazu discovers Bernoulli numbers

annotations.note: The Bernoulli numbers (bernoulli and bernoulli') are defined.

annotations.label: Taylor series

anchor.snippet: Brook Taylor develops the Taylor series

annotations.note: The Taylor polynomial/series is defined as taylorWithin (and Polynomial.taylor).

annotations.label: De Moivre's formula

anchor.snippet: states de Moivre's formula

annotations.note: Complex.cos_add_sin_mul_I_pow is de Moivre's formula.

annotations.label: Normal distribution

anchor.snippet: introduces the normal distribution to approximate the binomial distribution

annotations.note: The Gaussian (normal) measure on ℝ is defined as gaussianReal.

annotations.label: Basel problem

anchor.snippet: Leonhard Euler solves the Basel problem

annotations.note: hasSum_zeta_two proves ∑ 1/n² = π²/6.

annotations.label: Seven Bridges of Königsberg

anchor.snippet: solves the problem of the Seven Bridges of Königsberg

annotations.note: Eulerian-path graph theory is in Mathlib, but the specific Königsberg result (Konigsberg.not_isEulerian) lives in Archive, not the Mathlib library.

annotations.label: Linear ODE with constant coefficients

anchor.snippet: solves the general homogeneous linear ordinary differential equation with constant coefficients

annotations.note: The general constant-coefficient linear ODE solution is not formalized (Picard–Lindelöf existence is, but that's different).

annotations.label: Goldbach's conjecture

anchor.snippet: every even number greater than two can be expressed as the sum of two primes

annotations.note: Goldbach's conjecture is open and not stated/proven in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vibrating string problem

anchor.snippet: solves the vibrating string problem

annotations.note: The wave-equation vibrating-string problem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bayes' theorem

anchor.snippet: Thomas Bayes proves Bayes' theorem

annotations.note: Bayes' theorem relating conditional probabilities is formalized.

annotations.label: π is irrational

anchor.snippet: Johann Heinrich Lambert proves that π is irrational

annotations.note: irrational_pi states that π is irrational.

annotations.label: Divergence theorem

anchor.snippet: Joseph-Louis Lagrange discovers the divergence theorem

annotations.note: The divergence (Gauss–Ostrogradsky) theorem is formalized.

annotations.label: Constructibility of the regular 17-gon

anchor.snippet: the regular 17-gon can be constructed using only a compass and straightedge

annotations.note: Compass-and-straightedge constructibility (Gauss–Wantzel) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Prime number theorem (conjecture)

anchor.snippet: Adrien-Marie Legendre conjectures the prime number theorem

annotations.note: The prime-counting function is defined, but the PNT asymptotic is not in Mathlib core (only in the external PrimeNumberTheoremAnd project).

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of algebra

anchor.snippet: every polynomial equation has a solution among the complex numbers

annotations.note: Complex.exists_root (and Complex.isAlgClosed) is the fundamental theorem of algebra.

annotations.label: Abel–Ruffini theorem (partial)

anchor.snippet: quintic or higher equations cannot be solved by a general formula

annotations.note: The solvable-by-radicals framework and Galois-solvability direction are in Mathlib; the explicit unsolvable quintic is only in Archive.

annotations.label: Intermediate value theorem

anchor.snippet: a continuous function that is negative at one point and positive at another point must be zero

annotations.note: intermediate_value_Icc is the intermediate value theorem.

annotations.label: (ε, δ)-definition of limit

anchor.snippet: the first formal (ε, δ)-definition of limit

annotations.note: Mathlib provides the ε-δ characterizations of limits/continuity (Metric.tendsto_nhds_nhds, Metric.continuous_iff).

annotations.label: Pointwise limit of continuous functions (Cauchy's claim)

anchor.snippet: the pointwise limit of continuous functions is continuous

annotations.note: This claim is false in general; Mathlib instead has the correct version that uniform limits of continuous functions are continuous.

annotations.label: Cauchy's integral theorem

anchor.snippet: presents Cauchy's integral theorem

annotations.note: Cauchy's integral theorem/formula for differentiable complex functions is formalized.

annotations.label: Sophie Germain's theorem

anchor.snippet: Sophie Germain's Theorem is published

annotations.note: Sophie Germain's FLT case-1 theorem is not in Mathlib (the unrelated Sophie Germain algebraic identity is).

annotations.label: Abel–Ruffini theorem

anchor.snippet: the general quintic or higher equations cannot be solved by a general formula involving only arithmetical operations and roots

annotations.note: The radical-solvability framework is in Mathlib, but the explicit unsolvable quintic (exists_not_solvable_by_rad) is only in Archive.

annotations.label: Theory of residues

anchor.snippet: introduces the theory of residues in complex analysis

annotations.note: Complex-analytic residue calculus / the residue theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fermat's Last Theorem for n = 5

anchor.snippet: prove Fermat's Last Theorem for n = 5

annotations.note: Only the cases n=3 and n=4 of FLT are formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stokes' theorem

anchor.snippet: André-Marie Ampère discovers Stokes' theorem

annotations.note: The general/curl Kelvin–Stokes theorem isn't formalized, but the sibling divergence theorem is.

annotations.label: Counterexamples to Cauchy's continuity claim

anchor.snippet: gives counterexamples to

annotations.note: Abel's specific counterexamples to Cauchy's continuity claim are not formalized.

annotations.label: Green's theorem

anchor.snippet: George Green proves Green's theorem

annotations.note: Green's theorem (the 2D divergence theorem) is essentially the planar case of Mathlib's divergence theorem, but not stated under that name.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic non-Euclidean geometry

anchor.snippet: invent hyperbolic non-Euclidean geometry

annotations.note: Axiomatic hyperbolic geometry is not formalized in Mathlib (the upper half-plane exists but not hyperbolic geometry as such).

annotations.label: First proof of the divergence theorem

anchor.snippet: the first proof of the divergence theorem

annotations.note: The divergence theorem is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Solvability of algebraic equations (Galois)

anchor.snippet: presents a general condition for the solvability of algebraic equations

annotations.note: Galois theory and the radical-solvability criterion (solvable Galois group direction) are in Mathlib, but not the full Galois solvability iff.

annotations.label: Fermat's Last Theorem for n = 14

anchor.snippet: proves Fermat's Last Theorem for n = 14

annotations.note: FLT for n=14 is not formalized (only n=3 and n=4 are).

annotations.label: Dirichlet's theorem on primes in arithmetic progressions

anchor.snippet: Dirichlet's theorem about prime numbers in arithmetical progressions

annotations.note: Dirichlet's theorem on infinitely many primes in arithmetic progressions is formalized.

annotations.label: Impossibility of doubling the cube and trisecting the angle

anchor.snippet: doubling the cube and trisecting the angle are impossible with only a compass and straightedge

annotations.note: These compass-and-straightedge impossibility results are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Uniform convergence

anchor.snippet: First mention of uniform convergence

annotations.note: Uniform convergence is defined as TendstoUniformly.

annotations.label: Laurent expansion theorem

anchor.snippet: discovers and presents the Laurent expansion theorem

annotations.note: The analytic Laurent expansion theorem isn't formalized (formal LaurentSeries algebra is a different object).

annotations.label: Quaternions (non-commutative)

anchor.snippet: discovers the calculus of quaternions and deduces that they are non-commutative

annotations.note: Quaternions are defined (Quaternion / QuaternionAlgebra), with their noncommutative ring structure.

annotations.label: Boolean algebra

anchor.snippet: defining what is now called Boolean algebra

annotations.note: The BooleanAlgebra typeclass is defined.

annotations.label: Solitary waves from periodic waves

anchor.snippet: solitary waves can arise from a combination of periodic waves

annotations.note: Soliton/solitary-wave theory is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Essential singular points

anchor.snippet: introduces the concept of essential singular points

annotations.note: Classification of essential singularities (Casorati–Weierstrass) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Riemannian geometry

anchor.snippet: Bernhard Riemann introduces Riemannian geometry

annotations.note: Riemannian manifolds and bundles are defined (RiemannianBundle / IsRiemannianManifold).

annotations.label: Quaternions represent 4D rotations

anchor.snippet: quaternions can be used to represent rotations in four-dimensional space

annotations.note: Mathlib defines quaternions but not their representation of 4-dimensional rotations.

annotations.label: Möbius strip

anchor.snippet: August Ferdinand Möbius invents the Möbius strip

annotations.note: The Möbius strip is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: General quintic via elliptic/modular functions

anchor.snippet: solves the general quintic equation by means of elliptic and modular functions

annotations.note: Hermite's solution of the quintic via elliptic/modular functions is not formalized.

annotations.label: Riemann hypothesis

anchor.snippet: formulates the Riemann hypothesis

annotations.note: The Riemann hypothesis is formalized as a stated Prop (unproven).

annotations.label: Independence of the parallel postulate

anchor.snippet: from the other axioms of Euclidean geometry

annotations.note: Independence of the parallel postulate is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dedekind cut

anchor.snippet: invents what is now called the Dedekind Cut

annotations.note: Mathlib constructs ℝ via Cauchy sequences, not Dedekind cuts; no Dedekind-cut construction is present.

annotations.label: e is transcendental

anchor.snippet: Charles Hermite proves that e is transcendental

annotations.note: Transcendence of e is formalized only in an external project, not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Uncountability of the reals

anchor.snippet: the set of all real numbers is uncountably infinite

annotations.note: Cardinal.not_countable_real states the reals are uncountable.

annotations.label: π is transcendental

anchor.snippet: proves that π is transcendental

annotations.note: Transcendence of π (Lindemann) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Klein bottle

anchor.snippet: Felix Klein invents the Klein bottle

annotations.note: The Klein bottle is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Korteweg–de Vries equation

anchor.snippet: derive the Korteweg–de Vries equation

annotations.note: The KdV equation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Continuum hypothesis

anchor.snippet: the arithmetic of infinite cardinal numbers and the continuum hypothesis

annotations.note: Infinite cardinal arithmetic is fully formalized, but CH's independence is not in Mathlib (done externally in flypitch).

annotations.label: Prime number theorem (proof)

anchor.snippet: independently prove the prime number theorem

annotations.note: The PNT is proven in the external PrimeNumberTheoremAnd project, not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Exterior derivative

anchor.snippet: develops the exterior derivative

annotations.note: Cartan's exterior derivative of differential forms is not formalized (only exterior powers of modules exist).

annotations.label: Riemann problem (Plemelj)

anchor.snippet: solves the Riemann problem about the existence of a differential equation

annotations.note: The Riemann–Hilbert/Plemelj problem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Brouwer fixed-point theorem

anchor.snippet: presents the Brouwer fixed-point theorem

annotations.note: Brouwer's fixed-point theorem is formalized externally, not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Noether's symmetry theorem

anchor.snippet: every symmetry in physics has a corresponding conservation law

annotations.note: Noether's symmetry/conservation theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ramanujan conjecture

anchor.snippet: introduces Ramanujan conjecture

annotations.note: The Ramanujan conjecture is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Brun's constant

anchor.snippet: defines Brun's constant

annotations.note: Brun's constant (and Brun's theorem) are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: General definition of a commutative ring

anchor.snippet: the first general definition of a commutative ring

annotations.note: The CommRing typeclass is the general definition of a commutative ring.

annotations.label: Minimax theorem

anchor.snippet: proves the minimax theorem

annotations.note: The von Neumann/Sion minimax theorem is not formalized (minimaxRisk in decision theory is unrelated).

annotations.label: Three-cottage problem has no solution

anchor.snippet: the three-cottage problem has no solution

annotations.note: Graph planarity (K3,3 non-planarity) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gödel's incompleteness theorem

anchor.snippet: every axiomatic system for mathematics is either incomplete or inconsistent

annotations.note: Gödel's incompleteness theorems are formalized externally (FormalizedFormalLogic), not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Borsuk–Ulam theorem

anchor.snippet: present the Borsuk–Ulam antipodal-point theorem

annotations.note: The Borsuk–Ulam theorem is listed but not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Axiomatization of probability

anchor.snippet: an axiomatization of probability based on measure theory

annotations.note: Kolmogorov's measure-theoretic probability is formalized (Measure, IsProbabilityMeasure, ProbabilityTheory).

annotations.label: λ-calculus and Turing machine

anchor.snippet: formalizing the notion of computation and computability

annotations.note: Turing machines (Mathlib.Computability.TuringMachine) and computability via partial recursive functions are formalized; λ-calculus specifically is not.

annotations.label: Independence of CH and AC (consistency)

anchor.snippet: neither the continuum hypothesis nor the axiom of choice can be disproven

annotations.note: Gödel's relative consistency (constructible universe) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Arf invariant

anchor.snippet: Cahit Arf defines the Arf invariant

annotations.note: The Arf invariant of a quadratic form is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Eilenberg–Steenrod axioms

anchor.snippet: give the Eilenberg–Steenrod axioms

annotations.note: Mathlib has homology via category theory but not the Eilenberg–Steenrod axioms as such.

annotations.label: Weil conjectures

anchor.snippet: André Weil proposed his famous conjectures

annotations.note: The Weil conjectures are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complete list of uniform polyhedra

anchor.snippet: publish the complete list of uniform polyhedron

annotations.note: The classification of uniform polyhedra is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Existence of an exotic sphere

anchor.snippet: discovers the existence of an Exotic sphere in seven dimensions

annotations.note: Milnor's exotic 7-spheres are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Crease-free sphere eversion

anchor.snippet: the existence proof for crease-free sphere eversion

annotations.note: Sphere eversion is formalized in the external Sphere Eversion project, not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Grothendieck–Riemann–Roch theorem

anchor.snippet: the Grothendieck–Riemann–Roch theorem

annotations.note: The Grothendieck–Riemann–Roch theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Poincaré conjecture for dimensions ≥ 5

anchor.snippet: proves the Poincaré conjecture for all dimensions greater than or equal to 5

annotations.note: The high-dimensional Poincaré conjecture is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Independence of CH and AC (forcing)

anchor.snippet: neither the continuum hypothesis nor the axiom of choice can be proven

annotations.note: Cohen's forcing independence results are formalized externally (flypitch), not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Langlands program of conjectures

anchor.snippet: the influential Langlands program of conjectures

annotations.note: The Langlands program conjectures are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Atiyah–Singer index theorem

anchor.snippet: prove the Atiyah–Singer index theorem

annotations.note: The Atiyah–Singer index theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: No general algorithm for Diophantine equations (Hilbert's 10th)

anchor.snippet: there exists no general algorithm to solve all Diophantine equations

annotations.note: Diophantine sets and the Matiyasevich/Pell machinery are formalized, but the full DPRM undecidability conclusion is not.

annotations.label: Weil conjectures (Deligne's proof)

anchor.snippet: solves the last and deepest of the Weil conjectures

annotations.note: Deligne's proof of the Weil conjectures is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Four color theorem

anchor.snippet: prove the Four color theorem

annotations.note: The four color theorem is formalized in Coq, not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mordell conjecture

anchor.snippet: proves the Mordell conjecture

annotations.note: Faltings' theorem (Mordell conjecture) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Jones polynomial

anchor.snippet: discovers the Jones polynomial in knot theory

annotations.note: The Jones polynomial / knot theory is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bieberbach conjecture

anchor.snippet: proves the Bieberbach conjecture

annotations.note: De Branges' theorem (Bieberbach conjecture) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ribet's theorem

anchor.snippet: Ken Ribet proves Ribet's theorem

annotations.note: Ribet's theorem (epsilon conjecture) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Taniyama–Shimura conjecture and Fermat's Last Theorem

anchor.snippet: proves part of the Taniyama–Shimura conjecture and thereby proves Fermat's Last Theorem

annotations.note: Wiles' modularity result and the general FLT proof are not in Mathlib (formalization ongoing externally).

annotations.label: Bailey–Borwein–Plouffe formula

anchor.snippet: discovers Bailey–Borwein–Plouffe formula

annotations.note: The BBP formula for π is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kepler conjecture

anchor.snippet: proves the Kepler conjecture

annotations.note: The Kepler conjecture (Flyspeck) is formalized in HOL Light/Isabelle, not Mathlib.

annotations.label: Full Taniyama–Shimura conjecture

anchor.snippet: the full Taniyama–Shimura conjecture is proven

annotations.note: The full modularity theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: AKS primality test

anchor.snippet: an unconditional deterministic polynomial time algorithm to determine whether a given number is prime

annotations.note: The AKS primality test is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Catalan's conjecture

anchor.snippet: proves Catalan's conjecture

annotations.note: Mihăilescu's theorem (Catalan's conjecture) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Poincaré conjecture (Perelman)

anchor.snippet: Grigori Perelman proves the Poincaré conjecture

annotations.note: The 3-dimensional Poincaré conjecture / Ricci flow proof is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Classification of finite simple groups

anchor.snippet: the classification of finite simple groups

annotations.note: The classification of finite simple groups is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Green–Tao theorem

anchor.snippet: prove the Green–Tao theorem

annotations.note: The Green–Tao theorem on primes in arithmetic progressions is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fundamental lemma (Langlands program)

anchor.snippet: Fundamental lemma (Langlands program) is proved by

annotations.note: Ngô's fundamental lemma is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Erdős distinct distances problem

anchor.snippet: solve the Erdős distinct distances problem

annotations.note: The Guth–Katz distinct-distances result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: First finite bound on prime gaps

anchor.snippet: the first finite bound on gaps between prime numbers

annotations.note: Zhang's bounded prime gaps result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Erdős discrepancy problem

anchor.snippet: solves the Erdős discrepancy problem

annotations.note: The Erdős discrepancy theorem (Tao) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Graph isomorphism quasipolynomial algorithm

anchor.snippet: a quasipolynomial complexity algorithm would solve the Graph isomorphism problem

annotations.note: Babai's quasipolynomial graph-isomorphism algorithm is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sphere packing in dimension 8

anchor.snippet: solves the sphere packing problem in dimension 8

annotations.note: Viazovska's dimension-8 sphere packing result is not in Mathlib (formalization is a separate ongoing project).

annotations.label: Disproof of the Milnor conjecture

anchor.snippet: disprove the Milnor conjecture for six or more dimensions

annotations.note: The disproof of the Milnor conjecture (on diffeomorphism groups) is not formalized in Mathlib.
